promotion_state_machine.note: run_promotion with PASSING_GATE + CLEAN_SHADOW; clean_run never burns; rollback_run_burns_at_25 burns exactly at canary weight 25

drift_check.note: psi/ks/pvalue pinned against MIRROR's own serving/monitoring/drift.py, not this port; see mlops/tests/test_drift.py's module docstring for the exact independent-verification call
drift_check.fixture: np.linspace(0, 10, 50) reference vs np.linspace(0, 10, 50) [feature_a, unchanged] / np.linspace(5, 15, 50) [feature_b, +5 shift] current

tenant_rls.note: gate 5.4: sha256 of cdc/schema/tenancy.py's canonical_ddl() plus the one policy predicate every table uses; if either changes intentionally, update this AND docs/phases/PHASE_5.md in the same commit

tenant_slo_tiers.note: gate 5.4 golden cases: tier targets (real_time IS the Phase 1 table verbatim; near_real_time/batch are DESIGN targets, live measurement deferred to the W4 demo window) and the one-series-three-verdicts boundary case, verified against the real calculator

boundary_case.series: 1440 buckets at 60s, each total=1000 bad=30 (3% bad ratio, sustained)

ppo_stretch_expectations.note: gate 5.7 (LABELED STRETCH, never in the core pipeline): pins on tiny_synthetic_graph(). Changing the graph or coverage_minus_fuel updates this AND docs/phases/PHASE_5.md in the same commit. Namespaced so it is never confused with a core-pipeline checksum.

kl_crosscheck.note: AdaptiveKLController vendored bit-for-bit from pi-grpo; the test re-derives the update by the explicit formula and asserts equality on these inputs (same spirit as Phase 3's sklearn/scipy cross-checks).
